title: Method for fetal ultrasound image classification using pseudo-labelling with PCA-KMeans and an attention-augmented MobileNet-LSTM model
author: Shahade, Deshmukh, et al.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: 1. Accuracy and Loss curves while training:a. Accuracy Curve:

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Fig. 6 shows the accuracy curve. Validation starts high (nearly ∼97 percent) because frozen ImageNet MobileNet picks up useful features, while the training sits lower (∼93 percent). After this, only the attention + LSTM head learns, and the training accuracy achieves a high of 99 percent by epoch 4, leaving the validation to be near constant ∼98 percent. The consistent ∼1 percent gap and parallel trends signify learning with no over-fitting.b. Loss Curve:Fig. 6Accuracy curve.Fig. 6

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Fig. 7 shows the loss curve. The training loss decreases progressively from 0.17 to 0.03, validation loss slightly raises itself again after the first epoch when the new head settles down and then drops to an approximately value of 0.06, followed by a small raise at the tail-end. This indicates that improvements are harder to achieve, so it makes sense to stop early.Fig. 7Loss curve.Fig. 7

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Both curves show evidence of rapid convergence, a good alignment, without any apparent memorization and promising signs indicating a healthy training.2. The Final model showed the results ag given in the metric table:

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Table 3. Model performance metrics for fetal ultrasound image classification, showing accuracy, macro-precision, macro-recall, and macro-F1 score, all achieving 98 %. These metrics indicate high classification performance across all classes.3. Per-cluster performance:Table 3Model performance metrics.Table 3Accuracy98 %Macro-precision98 %Macro-recall98 %Macro-F198 %

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Clusters 0 & 1: Very high recall and precision (0.99 and ≥ 0.96) imply that the model almost never confuses these groups with others.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Cluster 2: perfect marks from all angles: this visual group is extremely distinctive for the network.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Cluster 3: Precision remains high at 0.99, whereas recall dropped to 0.94, meaning that ∼6 % of true-cluster-3 images are misclassified, generally as either cluster 0 or 1 (which can be seen in the confusion matrix). That minor shortcoming could be mitigated through focused data augmentation or re-weighting.
